title: Waymo DC permission and aggregate 2027 safety market drafts
warning: Draft only. No market has been created and no bet has been placed.

markets.question: Will Waymo be legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
Waymo says Waymo One is planned for Washington, DC in 2026, but also says policymakers still need to formalize regulations for operation without a human behind the wheel. Existing DC law establishes an autonomous vehicle testing program and requires DDOT approval for changes such as moving from an in-vehicle test operator to no test operator physically present. A related Manifold market already asks whether Washington DC will be a Waymo service city by June 30, 2026; this draft is narrower because it asks about legal permission for fully driverless paid public rides.

## Resolution
Resolve YES if, by 2026-12-31, DC law, regulation, DDOT permit approval, or another official DC government action legally permits Waymo to offer fully driverless paid ride-hailing service to public riders in Washington, DC with no human driver or test operator physically present in the vehicle. Resolve NO otherwise. Testing-only permission without paid public rides does not count.

Resolution date target: 2026-12-31

## Sources
- [Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.](https://waymo.com/blog/2025/03/next-stop-for-waymo-one-washingtondc/)
- [DC Law 23-156: Autonomous Vehicles Testing Program Amendment Act of 2020](https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/laws/23-156)
- [Axios: New D.C. Council bill would allow Waymo to begin robotaxi service](https://www.axios.com/local/washington-dc/2025/07/16/driverless-cars-dc-bill-waymo-robotaxis)
- [Manifold: Which new cities will Waymo serve by June 30?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/which-new-cities-will-waymo-serve-b)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 52
- Rationale: Waymo publicly targeted DC riders in 2026 and has strong incentive to secure approval, but DC policy approval and public-service authorization remain live gating items.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
markets.resolution_rule: Resolve YES if, by 2026-12-31, DC law, regulation, DDOT permit approval, or another official DC government action legally permits Waymo to offer fully driverless paid ride-hailing service to public riders in Washington, DC with no human driver or test operator physically present in the vehicle. Resolve NO otherwise. Testing-only permission without paid public rides does not count.

Manifold: Which new cities will Waymo serve by June 30?

notes: Draft only; review with local DC policy context before posting.
Initial probability is a seed forecast, not a market consensus.

markets.question: If Waymo is legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31, how many traffic fatalities will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
This is a conditional aggregate safety market, not a Waymo-involved crash market. The goal is to forecast whether permitting fully driverless Waymo service would be associated with lower or higher overall road-safety harm in DC during 2027. DC's tracked safety metrics include traffic fatalities, serious injuries, fatalities per vehicle mile traveled, pedestrian fatalities, bicyclist fatalities, and pedestrian/bicyclist injuries. This draft uses total traffic fatalities as the primary low-frequency but high-salience outcome.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If the condition is true, resolve to the number of traffic fatalities in DC during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Vision Zero, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Highway Safety Plan data. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.](https://waymo.com/blog/2025/03/next-stop-for-waymo-one-washingtondc/)
- [DC Vision Zero Traffic Fatalities and Injury Crashes](https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/district-of-columbia-vision-zero-traffic-fatalities-and-injury-crashes)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 38
- Rationale: DC's recent fatality baseline is in the low-to-mid 30s on multi-year averages, but year-to-year variance is high. If Waymo is permitted, any aggregate effect in 2027 is likely modest because deployment scale and substitution from human-driven trips are uncertain.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
markets.resolution_rule: Resolve N/A if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

notes: This market estimates aggregate DC safety, not Waymo crash involvement.
Pair with the not-permitted conditional market to compare expected citywide outcomes.

markets.question: If Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31, how many traffic fatalities will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
This is the counterpart to the permitted conditional market. It estimates DC's aggregate 2027 road-safety outcome if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public rides by the end of 2026.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if Waymo is legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If the condition is true, resolve to the number of traffic fatalities in DC during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Vision Zero, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Highway Safety Plan data. If sources differ, prefer the finalized DC Vision Zero/DDOT value; if unavailable by the resolution date target, use the latest official DC government or FARS value available and document the source used.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [DC Vision Zero Traffic Fatalities and Injury Crashes](https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/district-of-columbia-vision-zero-traffic-fatalities-and-injury-crashes)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 40
- Rationale: Without permitted Waymo service, 2027 fatalities should mostly follow DC's existing traffic-safety trend and annual volatility. The estimate is slightly above the permitted case because a permitted service could substitute for some higher-risk human driving, but the expected difference is small.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._

markets.question: If Waymo is legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31, how many serious injuries in traffic crashes will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
Traffic fatalities are highly salient but noisy. Serious injuries are a broader aggregate safety metric that DC uses in highway safety planning and crash reporting. This conditional market asks whether DC's total serious-injury count is lower or higher in the world where Waymo is legally permitted to operate fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides by the end of 2026.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If the condition is true, resolve to the number of serious injuries in DC traffic crashes during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Highway Safety Plan, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Vision Zero data. If the source labels the metric as major injuries or KABCO A injuries, use that official serious-injury-equivalent count.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.](https://waymo.com/blog/2025/03/next-stop-for-waymo-one-washingtondc/)
- [DC Crashes in DC metadata](https://opendata.dc.gov/documents/DCGIS::crashes-in-dc/about)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 315
- Rationale: DC's recent serious-injury baseline is in the low 300s. A permitted Waymo launch could slightly reduce risk if it substitutes for human-driven trips, but 2027 deployment scale and changes in total vehicle miles traveled are uncertain.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
markets.resolution_rule: Resolve N/A if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If the condition is true, resolve to the number of serious injuries in DC traffic crashes during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Highway Safety Plan, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Vision Zero data. If the source labels the metric as major injuries or KABCO A injuries, use that official serious-injury-equivalent count.

sources.title: Waymo: Next stop for Waymo One: Washington, D.C.

source_forecast.rationale: DC's recent serious-injury baseline is in the low 300s. A permitted Waymo launch could slightly reduce risk if it substitutes for human-driven trips, but 2027 deployment scale and changes in total vehicle miles traveled are uncertain.

notes: This market estimates aggregate DC safety, not Waymo crash involvement.
Use the same official serious-injury definition across both conditional markets.

markets.question: If Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31, how many serious injuries in traffic crashes will DC record in calendar year 2027?
markets.description_markdown:
## Context
This is the serious-injury counterpart for the not-permitted condition. It estimates the aggregate citywide safety outcome if Waymo is not legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public rides by the end of 2026.

## Resolution
Resolve N/A if Waymo is legally permitted to offer fully driverless paid public robotaxi rides in Washington, DC by 2026-12-31. If the condition is true, resolve to the number of serious injuries in DC traffic crashes during calendar year 2027 according to finalized official DC Highway Safety Plan, DDOT, MPD, FARS, or DC Vision Zero data. If the source labels the metric as major injuries or KABCO A injuries, use that official serious-injury-equivalent count.

Resolution date target: 2028-03-31

## Sources
- [DC Crashes in DC metadata](https://opendata.dc.gov/documents/DCGIS::crashes-in-dc/about)
- [DC FY24-26 Highway Safety Plan](https://www.nhtsa.gov/sites/nhtsa.gov/files/2024-01/DC_FY24-26HSP-tag.pdf)

## Seed forecast
- Point estimate: 323
- Rationale: Without permitted Waymo service, serious injuries should mostly follow DC's existing trend. The estimate is close to recent multi-year averages and slightly above the permitted condition, but the expected Waymo-specific aggregate effect in 2027 is small relative to baseline variance.

_Drafted by farness. Review wording and resolution criteria before posting._
